questions.question: Which stream class is used to write to files?
questions.answer: `std::ofstream`

questions.question: Which stream class is used to read from files?
questions.answer: `std::ifstream`

questions.question: Which header provides file stream classes?
questions.answer: `<fstream>`

questions.question: How do you check if a file failed to open?
questions.answer: `if (!file)`

questions.question: What does `std::fstream` allow?
questions.answer: Both read and write

questions.question: What function reads an entire line from a file into a string?
questions.answer: `std::getline(file, line)`

questions.question: If `std::ofstream` opens an existing file without any flags, what happens?
questions.answer: Truncates (overwrites) the file

questions.question: To append to an existing file use:
questions.answer: `std::ofstream f("file.txt", std::ios::app)`

questions.question: When is a file object's destructor called?
options: When the object goes out of scope — closing the file automatically
questions.answer: When the object goes out of scope — closing the file automatically

questions.question: What does `outFile.close()` do?
options: Flushes and closes the file stream
questions.answer: Flushes and closes the file stream

questions.question: Which flag opens a file in binary mode?
questions.answer: `std::ios::binary`

questions.question: What does `write(reinterpret_cast<char*>(&x), sizeof(x))` do?
options: Writes raw bytes of x to the file
questions.answer: Writes raw bytes of x to the file

questions.question: What is `std::stringstream`?
options: An in-memory stream that works like a file but in RAM
questions.answer: An in-memory stream that works like a file but in RAM

questions.question: What does `ss.str()` return?
options: The string built in the stringstream
questions.answer: The string built in the stringstream

questions.question: What does `file.seekg(0, std::ios::end)` do?
options: Seeks read position to one past the last byte
questions.answer: Seeks read position to one past the last byte

questions.question: What does `file.tellg()` return?
questions.answer: Current read position in bytes

questions.question: To get file size using seekg/tellg:
questions.answer: Seek to end, call tellg()

questions.question: What does `std::ios::ate` flag do when opening?
options: Seek to end immediately after opening
questions.answer: Seek to end immediately after opening

questions.question: What does `file.eof()` return?
options: true when read past end of file
true when file is just opened
questions.answer: true when read past end of file

questions.question: What does `file.read(buffer, n)` do?
options: Reads up to n bytes into buffer
questions.answer: Reads up to n bytes into buffer

questions.question: What is memory-mapped I/O?
options: Mapping file contents directly to virtual address space — avoids read/write syscalls per access
questions.answer: Mapping file contents directly to virtual address space — avoids read/write syscalls per access

questions.question: Which C++17 header provides portable file system operations?
questions.answer: `<filesystem>`

questions.question: What does `std::filesystem::exists(p)` do?
options: Returns true if path p exists on the filesystem
questions.answer: Returns true if path p exists on the filesystem

questions.question: What does `fs::directory_iterator` iterate over?
questions.answer: All entries in a directory

questions.question: What is `std::filesystem::file_size(p)`?
options: Returns the size of a file in bytes
questions.answer: Returns the size of a file in bytes

questions.question: What is the difference between text and binary mode file I/O?
options: Text mode translates '\n' on Windows (CRLF); binary mode doesn't — important for portable code
questions.answer: Text mode translates '\n' on Windows (CRLF); binary mode doesn't — important for portable code

questions.question: What is `std::filesystem::recursive_directory_iterator`?
options: Recursively traverses all files and directories under a given path
questions.answer: Recursively traverses all files and directories under a given path

questions.question: What does `fs::path::extension()` return?
questions.answer: The file extension (e.g., .txt)

questions.question: What is `std::ofstream::flush()` vs `close()`?
options: flush() writes buffered data without closing; close() flushes and closes
questions.answer: flush() writes buffered data without closing; close() flushes and closes

questions.question: What does `std::ios_base::failure` exception indicate?
options: A stream I/O error — thrown when `exceptions()` is enabled on a stream and an error occurs
questions.answer: A stream I/O error — thrown when `exceptions()` is enabled on a stream and an error occurs
